vocab.example: Please mute yourself when you're not speaking.
vocab.example_vi: Vui lòng tắt tiếng khi bạn không nói.

vocab.example: Can you screen share your slides for the meeting?
vocab.example_vi: Bạn chia sẻ màn hình slide cho cuộc họp được không?

vocab.example: Sorry, my connection is a bit unstable today.
vocab.example_vi: Xin lỗi, kết nối mạng của tôi hơi không ổn định hôm nay.

vocab.example: Let's follow the agenda to save time.
vocab.example_vi: Hãy theo chương trình họp để tiết kiệm thời gian.

vocab.example: Please check the time zone before you join the call.
vocab.example_vi: Vui lòng kiểm tra múi giờ trước khi tham gia cuộc gọi.

vocab.example: Sorry to interrupt, but can I ask a quick question?
vocab.example_vi: Xin lỗi vì ngắt lời, nhưng tôi hỏi nhanh được không?

vocab.example: There is a lot of background noise on your line.
vocab.example_vi: Có nhiều tiếng ồn xung quanh trên đường truyền của bạn.

vocab.example: Let's wrap up the meeting in five minutes.
vocab.example_vi: Hãy kết thúc cuộc họp trong năm phút nữa.

listening.title: A remote meeting with connection issues

lines.speaker: Hoa
lines.text: Good morning everyone. Let's follow the agenda to save time.

lines.speaker: Minh
lines.text: Sorry, my connection is a bit unstable today.

lines.speaker: Hoa
lines.text: No problem. Can everyone please mute themselves when not speaking?

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: Sure. There is some background noise on my line too, sorry about that.

lines.speaker: Hoa
lines.text: That's okay. Minh, can you screen share your report now?

lines.speaker: Minh
lines.text: Yes, one second... Sorry to interrupt, but can everyone see my screen?

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: Yes, we can see it clearly.

lines.speaker: Hoa
lines.text: Great. Also, remember our client is in a different time zone, so let's keep this short.

lines.speaker: Minh
lines.text: Understood. I'll go through the main points quickly.

lines.speaker: Hoa
lines.text: Thank you. Let's wrap up the meeting in five minutes.

questions.q: What problem does Minh have at the start?
What does Hoa ask everyone to do?
Why does Hoa want to keep the meeting short?
options: The client is in a different time zone

shadowing: Let's follow the agenda closely today, so we can save some time.
Please mute yourself when you're not speaking to avoid background noise.
Sorry to interrupt, but can everyone see my screen clearly right now?
Remember our client is in a different time zone, so let's be quick.
Let's wrap up the meeting in five minutes and send a short summary.

interview.question: What do you think makes a remote meeting effective?
tips_vi: Nêu 2-3 yếu tố cụ thể: agenda rõ ràng, đúng giờ, hạn chế gián đoạn
Có thể nhắc đến việc tôn trọng múi giờ của người tham gia
Kết nối với kinh nghiệm thực tế nếu có
interview.sample_answer: I think a good remote meeting starts with a clear agenda, so everyone knows what to expect and the meeting doesn't run too long. It also helps when people mute themselves when they're not speaking, to avoid background noise, since a noisy connection can distract the whole team. I always try to check the time zone of everyone joining, especially when working with international clients, so no one has to join at a strange hour. During the call, I think it's important to give everyone a chance to speak and avoid interrupting, even if the connection is not perfect. Finally, ending on time and sending a short summary afterward makes the meeting more useful for everyone, because people can review the main points later instead of relying only on memory.

interview.question: How do you handle technical problems, like a bad connection, during an important online meeting?
tips_vi: Nêu cách xử lý thực tế: thông báo sớm, dùng chat, có phương án dự phòng
Thể hiện thái độ bình tĩnh, chuyên nghiệp
Có thể kể một ví dụ cụ thể
interview.sample_answer: Once my internet connection became very unstable during an important client meeting, right when I was about to present my report. I immediately let everyone know through the chat, so they understood what was happening and didn't think I had simply left the call. I turned off my camera to save bandwidth and focused on staying connected by audio, which helped a lot. When the connection was still too weak after a few minutes, I called in using my phone as a backup, and a colleague kindly shared my screen for me. After the meeting, I sent a follow-up email with a summary and the slides, to make sure I didn't miss any important points that the client needed. I think staying calm and communicating clearly is the key in these situations, because panicking usually makes the problem feel worse than it really is.
